libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. For test-code setup, update any custom AttributeImpl test subclass to implement public void reflectWith(AttributeReflector reflector), emitting the attribute fields expected by the test. Keep coverage focused on in-process Lucene core APIs such as RAMDirectory, analyzers, codecs/SPI loading, IndexWriter/DirectoryReader, AttributeSource/AttributeImpl behavior, and RamUsageEstimator. If JVM tests exercise Lucene internals that access java.nio or jdk.internal.ref on modern JDKs, preserve the existing --add-opens JVM arguments used by prior lucene-core tests.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #5851 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported javac failure is caused by a source-incompatible Lucene 6 API change, not missing external setup.
Meaningful native-image coverage may still require adapting tests to Lucene 6 behavior and service-provider resources, but local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed; lucene-core 6.0.0 is a standalone jar, but generated tests that subclass AttributeImpl must account for the Lucene 6 API change making reflectWith(AttributeReflector) abstract.
